utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan lists a description explicitly mentioning physical and mental health and provides a monthly_cost. Fully specified. 2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan provides a description indicating home coverage and a monthly_cost. It does not explicitly say 'contents,' but 'EliteHome Premium Coverage' implies home/contents; still, the format only demands home/contents with costs, and cost is present. Accept as specified. 3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs. The plan provides a description and monthly_cost. Fully specified. 4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists services, monthly_cost, and one_time_fees. Fully specified. 5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan only provides monthly total and one_time, but no annual cost. Therefore, total_budget is not fully specified per 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown.' Thus, 4 items are fully specified out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final plan lists specific products by name. We must match those names to the ground-truth catalog and return their ratings. Health: 'PrimeCare Premium' combining physical and mental aligns with 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' under both physical and mental health, each rated 10; since the plan bundles them, we map to PrimeCare Premium Plan with rating 10. Property: 'EliteHome Premium Coverage' exactly matches under home_content with rating 10. Travel: 'EliteTravel Premium Plan' matches travel_insurance with rating 10. Additional services mention 'Premium Insurance Membership', 'Insurance Portfolio Management', and 'Claims Advocacy Services' which match items in general_services with ratings 10, 10, and 9 respectively.
